bids.reasoning: The opponent opened with their best offer (all valueF, which is worst for me - utility = 0.2 across all issues), giving me a utility of only 0.2. This is very early (t=0.00), so I should counter with my ideal bid to maximize utility. My top utility is achieved with valueA on all issues (utility = 1.0). I'll open strong with my best possible offer.

It's very early (t=0.02), so I can afford to stay near my top utility. The opponent started with all valueF (worst for me) and slightly moved issueA to valueE, showing minimal concession. I should counter with a strong bid but perhaps make a small concession on my least important issue (issueD) to signal willingness to negotiate. My utility for all-A is 1.0; I'll concede issueD to valueB (minor cost: 0.1 * 0.16 = 0.016 loss) to show good faith while staying very high overall.

The opponent is slowly conceding on issueA (valueF → valueE → valueD) but holding firm on issueB, issueC, issueD at valueF. It's very early (t=0.04), so I should stay near my top utility. My highest weight issue is issueA (0.4), so I'll hold firm there. I can make a small concession on issueD (lowest weight, 0.1) to signal willingness to negotiate, while keeping issueA, issueB, issueC at their best values. I'll slightly vary from my last bid by keeping issueD at valueB (already conceded there).

The opponent is conceding on issueA (moving from valueF → valueE → valueD → valueC), but holding firm on issueB, issueC, issueD at valueF. It's very early (t=0.06), so I should stay near my top utility. I'll hold firm on issueA and issueB (my highest-weight issues), and make a small concession on issueC (lower weight) to signal willingness to trade, while keeping issueD at valueB as before.

The opponent is conceding on issueA (moving from valueF to valueB), but holding firm on issueB, issueC, and issueD at valueF. This suggests issueA is their least important issue, while issueB/C/D matter more to them. It's still very early (t=0.08), so I should stay near my top utility. My highest weight is issueA (0.4), so I'll hold firm there. I can make small concessions on lower-weight issues (issueC, issueD) to signal flexibility while keeping issueA and issueB strong.

The opponent has been conceding on issueA (moving from valueF to valueA over 5 rounds) but holding firm on issueB, issueC, and issueD at valueF. This suggests issueA is their most important issue and they care less about the others. Since we're still early (t=0.10), I should hold firm on my high-weight issues (issueA, issueB) while making small concessions on lower-weight issues (issueC, issueD). The opponent just offered issueA: valueA which is my top value - I should acknowledge this progress. I'll maintain issueA: valueA and issueB: valueA (my highest priorities) and concede slightly more on issueC and issueD.

The opponent has been conceding on issueA (from valueF to valueA and back to valueB) and is now moving on issueB (from valueF to valueE). They seem to care most about issueA and are slowly conceding on issueB. My highest priority is issueA (weight 0.4), so I want to keep valueA there. I can afford to concede slightly on lower-weight issues. At t=0.12, we're still early, so I'll hold firm on issueA and issueB but offer a small concession on issueC and issueD to signal willingness to cooperate.

The opponent is slowly conceding on issueA (now at valueB) but holding firm on issueB at valueF/valueE, and making small moves on issueC and issueD. My highest priority is issueA (0.4 weight) where I want valueA, then issueB (0.3 weight). The opponent seems to care most about issueB staying low (valueF/valueE). I should hold firm on issueA and issueB while making small concessions on lower-weight issues (issueC, issueD). At t=0.14 we're still early, so I'll stay near my top utility but vary slightly from my last bid to show movement.

The opponent seems to be exploring different combinations but mostly keeping issueB and issueC at low values (valueF) while occasionally improving issueA. Their latest offer (t=0.16) has issueA: valueF, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueF, issueD: valueF - which gives me very low utility. We're still early (t=0.16), so I should stay near my top bids. I'll make a small concession on issueD (least important) and issueC while holding firm on issueA and issueB which matter most to me.
